observations.recommendation:
We recommend to the management that the Municipal Accountant shall withhold the salaries and other emoluments due to the concerned accountable officers until the same have been fully settled/liquidated. For national government agency employees, the Municipal Treasurer should furnish their Regional Office with the demand letters.
File the request for authority to COA to write-off of dormant receivable accounts,unliquidated cash advances and fund transfers of National Government Agencies (NGAs), Local Government Units (LGUs) and Government-Owned and Controlled Corporation (GOCCs)in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016 duly supported with schedule of dormant accounts certified by the Accountant and relevant documents validating the existence of the conditions, as applicable.
As for those who already retired/resigned from the service, make a final demand for its settlement to be sent to their present addresses or new official stations.

We recommend management to require the Inventory Committee to conduct physical count of its road networks in order to ascertain the existence of the balance of the account Road Networks.
Moreover, direct the Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant of the complete description and cost segregation of the road networks to facilitate the preparation of the subsidiary ledgers for roads and its components. Likewise, require the General Services Officer to prepare and maintain the Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components, including Road Map. A Report on Local Road Network is to be prepared by the General Services Officer at the end of accounting period.

We recommend that management shall intensify its collection capability in order to at least achieve its targeted revenue. Also, the Report on Estimated Revenue shall be revised if necessary, based on the historical data in order to establish a realizable estimated revenue.
With regards to the giving of free rentals, the Municipal Ordinance No.07, s. 2015 shall be amended in order to provide a clause or provision granting the samein case circumstances may arise that is/are beyond the control of the municipality so that any actions taken by the management shall be in accordance with laws, rules and regulations.

It is recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
1. To recognize the LDDRMF current year appropriation under the General Fund books. Transfer only the unexpended/unspent DRRM funds at the end of the year to the Trust Fund so that all disbursements chargeable against the general fund shall be properly reflected under the same fund in order to present a fairly stated financial statements of all funds.
2.To effect necessary adjustments in the books to reflect the transactions in their          appropriate/corrected accounts/funds as discussed above.

We recommend that the Local Development Council and the Municipal Engineer should closely monitor and pursue the timely and full implementation of all the projectsfunded under BUB and LGSF and to submit regular status reports to the Municipal Mayor for his information so that appropriate measures can be instituted in the event projects cannot be fully implemented as planned.

We recommend that management, members of the Solid Waste Management Board (SWMB) shall exercise their duties and responsibilities as what has been contemplated in the 10 year SWM Plan to ensure the effective implementation of SWM Programs.
The SWM Board shall also oversee the execution of SWM operations so as to determine whether practices required under R.A. 9003 which are not being followed and lapses in SWM operation as well can be immediately noted and corrected.

We recommend that records from the Accounting and Treasurer’s Office pertaining to previous shares of RPT and CTC collection intended for the barangays shall be retrieved and reconciled in order to establish a detailed schedule showing thereof actual shares for each barangay and be remitted in accordance with Section 271 (d) of RA 7160.
However, if reconciliation cannot be made possible due to absence of prior data and related records, such can be remitted to all barangays proportionately based on historical data of collected shares of RPT and CTC per barangays.

We recommend that management shall conduct the gender analysis and sex-disaggregated data for the municipality to ensure that in the preparation stage of the GAD plan forprograms, projects and activities shall truly address a well-defined gender issues towards the achievement of the Philippine Plan for Gender-Responsive Development.
